intro: ^It is the dawn of the Edo era.
^Tokugawa Ieyasu has crushed his last great rival at Sekigahara.
^His rule is new, and the land has not yet caught up with it —
^roving bandits and masterless samurai move through a country
^The warriors of Japan are learning a new game: not glory on
^the battlefield, but honor and standing under the Shogun.
^Many seek employment by proving themselves against the martial
^schools that dot the road between every village and city.
^You have two swords, the clothes on your back, and no master.
road_encounter: ^The road narrowed between old pines and a man sat in the dust with his back against a stone eating cold rice from a cloth. He did not look up. He was old and one of his sandals lay beside him in the road, the strap split clean through.

c-0: ^You went past him on the far side of the road. He watched you down the track without turning his head.
c-1: ^You stopped. He looked up. It was the face of a man for whom the worst has already arranged itself and been accepted.
^He held up the sandal. That was all.

c-0: ^You crouched and laced the strap with cord from your pack. He watched your hands.
^"You have done this before," he said. "For someone."
^You didn't answer. You stood and walked on.
c-1: ^You looked at the sandal. You looked at him. You walked on.

c-2: ^He moved. He did not look at you again.

river_crossing: ^The road gave out onto a crossing of pale stones. The water ran cold and clear over them and on the far bank a boy sat with a line in the current.

b:
^He looked up when you were midstream.

^"That old man back on the south road. That was my grandfather. He said a swordsman fixed his sandal."

^He held out a cloth parcel. Rice and pickled plum.

^"He said to wait here," the boy said.

^You took it. You crossed without speaking.

^The boy glanced at you and put his eyes back on the water.

^You crossed. The river ran on behind you the same as it had before.

^The boy saw you coming. He gathered his things without hurrying and moved further down the bank.

^You crossed alone. The water was cold. On the far side the road continued as if nothing had happened on it.

pass_warned: ^A merchant coming south stopped when he saw you. He didn't waste words.
^"Three men at the pass since morning. There's a woman."
^He went. You left the road and moved through the cedar line, coming up on the pass from above. From there you could see the shape of it before they saw you.
pass_hostile_entry: ^Three men in the road. A woman with her back to the rock.
^They saw you coming and the one who held her didn't let go — he turned toward you instead.
^"There he is," one of them said. "The swordsman who put old Kenji in the dirt on the south road."
^The woman looked at you. She couldn't tell yet what you were.
^"Your reputation," the leader said. "Pay for it or bleed for it."

c-1: ^You said it flat. No ceremony to it.
^"Your purse then," he said finally. "We'll call it even."

c-0: ^You set the purse in the road between you.
^He picked it up without taking his eyes off you. They left. The woman watched them go and then she looked at you, trying to work out what she'd witnessed.
c-1: ^He considered you a long time. Then he looked at the woman. Then back.
^Whether it was the words or something else in your face you'll never know.

c-2: ^He took your silence for what it was.

pass_cold_entry: ^Three men in the road. A woman with her back to the rock and a hand at her collar. She had given up fighting somewhere behind where you found her.
^The man doing the holding was watching you come.

b: ^You were above them. They hadn't seen you yet. You could end it before it started.

pass_scene: ^Watch. Wait for the shape of it.

b:
^You stepped into view already drawn.

^They spun toward you and found a sword at the distance where decisions get made fast.

^The calculation didn't take long.

^You didn't speak. You drew and closed half the distance.

^He read what he saw.

c-0: ^He let the woman go and stepped back.
^"Not worth it," he said. To the others or to himself.
^They left. The woman watched them go and then she looked at you.
^You sheathed your sword. Nothing had happened here.
c-1: ^He looked at you and made a calculation.
^One of the others moved to flank you. You turned toward him and the leader took that moment to swing.
c-2: ^The word landed wrong. He swung at the woman to show you he wasn't afraid.

pass_fight: ^Go through them. Don't stop to count.

c-0: ^Two of them went down without dying. The third ran.
^The woman was free. The man who had held her sat in the dirt breathing with his mouth open, looking at nothing in particular.
c-1: ^You went through all three. More than was needed.
^The woman was free. The road was quiet in the way roads go quiet after.

pass_runner: ^One of them was already moving — scrambling up the embankment, not looking back.

c-0: ^You watched him clear the ridge and disappear into the tree line.
c-1: ^When you came back the woman was gone.

pass_surrender: ^The man who had held the woman was still on the ground. He looked up at you.
^He was not a warrior. Hunger has a particular face and he was wearing it.

c-0: ^You walked past. The woman was already gone.
c-1: ^You said it without looking at him.
^You heard him get to his feet and walk the other direction.

pass_aftermath_clean: ^The woman adjusted her collar. She looked at you the way people look when they don't know what to do with what they feel.
pass_aftermath_bloody: ^The woman was gone. You didn't know when she'd left.
^The road was quiet. Your hands were steady.
^That was the part that stayed with you.
pass_continues: ^The pass opened onto a wide valley. A line of cedars marked the road going north and the light was failing and there was an inn at the base of the slope.
roadside_inn: ^The inn was small. A low table. A single lantern. Your swords close at hand as they always were.

b:
^The serving girl came to refill your cup and leaned close before she poured.

^"The men in the next room," she said. "They've been asking about the swordsman traveling alone."

^She went before you could answer.

roadside_inn: ^Through the paper wall, voices. Low. Unhurried.
^"How much do you think he's carrying."
^You ate. You didn't look at the wall.
^It moved across the paper slow as weather — the shape of a man bent low, trying not to make sound. It stopped outside your door.
^A finger appeared at the edge of the panel. It pressed and rotated until a small dark circle opened in the paper.
inn_do_nothing: ^You ate. The eye watched. You gave it nothing to read.
^A silence longer than the one before. Then the sound of men deciding what your stillness meant.
^They decided it meant you hadn't noticed.

^You woke to grey light and a room that had been gone through in the night. Your pack open. Your coin purse taken. They had left your swords — not from mercy, you thought. From some understanding of what morning would bring if they hadn't.
^You lay still a while looking at the ceiling.
inn_demonstration: ^You paused your meal without looking at the wall.
^Two flies had been circling the lantern. You watched them without appearing to watch them. Your chopsticks poised.
^Then you reached out and plucked them from the air.
^Clean. Unhurried. The way you might lift a cup.
^You set each one down on the tatami by your knees and went back to eating your rice.
^The eye at the hole didn't move for a long time.
^Shuffling sounds. A word too low to make out. Then nothing.
inn_speak: ^You stood. You didn't draw. You faced the wall.
^The eye disappeared. On the other side, the sound of a calculation being revised.
^"Take what's on the table and go."
^Nothing else. Let the silence do it.

c-0: ^You set your coin purse where the hole could see it.
^A moment. Then a hand came under the gap at the bottom of the wall and took it.
^You sat back down. The rice had gone cold.
c-1: ^The wall was thin. They knew what was on this side of it and they knew that you knew.
^Shuffling. Then footsteps moving away. The outer door opened and closed.

inn_preemptive: ^You drew in one motion and stepped through the paper wall.
^They were not ready for that. No one ever is.
^It was over before it had properly started. More quickly than it needed to be.
^The innkeeper appeared in the doorway. He looked at what was on the floor. He looked at you.
^You cleaned your sword. You left coin on the table — more than the room cost. It didn't cover what you'd done but it was what you had.
inn_morning: ^In the morning the inn was quiet.

b:
^You had nothing for the innkeeper. He looked at your face and waved you off without a word. He'd seen this before.

^You stepped into the road feeling lighter than you had any right to. Not free. Just empty.
^You left coin on the table. The serving girl was already up. She watched you go from the doorway and said nothing.
^The road felt different in the morning. Or you did.

inn_morning_early: ^You left before dawn. The road was empty.
^Behind you a light came on in the inn.

b: ^The road north. No coin. Your swords. You had had less before. You tried to remember when.
^The road north opened ahead of you, unhurried. Something had settled in you in the night and you weren't sure when it happened.
^The road north. Lighter by a purse. Peace costs something. It usually does.
^The road north. You walked quickly and did not look back.

inn_departs: ^Somewhere ahead, above the treeline, the curved roof of a temple you had heard about.
hozoin_approach: ^The road found the temple before you expected it. A curved roof through the cedars and the smell of cold incense and a bell that had already gone silent. At the gate a young monk stood and watched you come up the path. He didn't speak. He was deciding something about you.
hozoin_entry_compassionate: ^The young monk bowed and led you without being asked.

^Not to the practice yard. To a small room looking out over the garden where an old man sat with two cups of tea already poured. He was small and bent and had the eyes of a man who had been watching the road for several days.
^"I heard you helped an old man on the south road," he said. "And a woman at the mountain pass."
^He gestured to the cup across from him.
^"Sit. There is something I want to tell you before you meet my student."
hozoin_entry_middle: ^The young monk led you to the practice yard without ceremony.
^An old man stood at the far edge of the courtyard. He didn't introduce himself. He watched you arrive the way a man watches weather he has been expecting.
^At the center of the yard a young monk was working through drills with a long spear. He moved the way water moves — no decision visible between one position and the next.
^He set down the shorter spear and picked up the longer one.
hozoin_entry_cruel: ^The monk at the gate looked at your swords for a long time before he stepped aside.
^He led you to the practice yard quickly, like a man completing an obligation he wanted finished.
^The yard was empty except for one figure. Seventeen, perhaps. He was holding a spear as long as he was tall and he was looking at you with an expression the monk clearly did not like.
hozoin_abbot_full_audience: ^His name was In'ei. He had been the finest spear fighter in the province once. Now he poured tea with hands that shook slightly and he watched you the way he once watched men who came at him meaning to do damage.
^"He was left at our gate in winter," he said. "An infant. We thought he wouldn't survive the cold."
^He paused. Not for effect. He was living in the memory for a moment before he came back.
^"He took to the spear the way some children take to breathing. I cannot explain it. I have stopped trying."
^He turned his cup slowly in his hands.
^"He has beaten every monk in this temple. He is seventeen years old. He does not know what to do with that."

c-0: ^You listened. He took this as understanding.
^"A boy like this," he said. "Too much talent, too soon, too little of what softens a person. You know what I am describing."
^"If nothing humbles him he will lead this temple into ruin. He will pick fights with men he cannot beat. He will believe his spear is proof of something it is not proof of."
^"I need someone to humble him. Not destroy him. There is a difference."
c-1: ^"Because you are the first person who has come up that road in a long time who might understand the difference between beating a man and breaking him."

hozoin_abbot_the_gamble: ^"I will not pretend this is without risk," he said. "He may hurt you badly. Or worse."
^He stood slowly, with the effort of a man who does this less easily than he once did.
^"He is the closest thing I have to a son."
^A long silence. The garden outside. A bird somewhere in the cedar.
^"I am making a very dangerous wager with something I cannot afford to lose."
^"Come. I will take you to him."
hozoin_fight_begins_with_knowledge: ^Inshun was already there. He had been waiting — not impatiently. He was incapable of impatience. He was simply present, the way a spear left standing in the ground is present.
^He looked at you. He looked at In'ei. Something passed between them that you were not part of.
^Then he turned back to you and raised the spear.
hozoin_fight_begins: ^His face was still becoming what it would be. But his hands on the spear were something else entirely — certain in a way that had nothing to do with age, as if they had arrived in the world already knowing.
hozoin_the_fight: ^He was fast. Faster than anyone you had met.
^Not because he was trying to be fast. He was fast the way falling is fast — the most direct path, no deliberation, nothing wasted.
^Three exchanges and you understood you were not fighting a student.

c-0: ^He read it before you moved. The spear found the angle you hadn't accounted for.
^You were good. He was something else.
c-1: ^He was not impatient. He would wait as long as you would and he would be more rested at the end of it.
c-2: ^You had fought spear fighters before. The spear is useless at close range.
^You closed the distance in one step —
^— and found that he had anticipated it. He reversed the spear before you finished the thought.
^The butt of it caught you across the chest.

hozoin_recognition_moment: ^Between one movement and the next you saw it.
^In his eyes. Something with no name — not rage, not hunger. Something older than both. Something that had been present since before he had language for it.
^You had felt this. You knew where it came from.

c-0: ^He didn't press the advantage. He was waiting to see what this was.
^You looked at him — not at the spear, at him — and you understood something you had not come here to understand.
^He fought because it was everything he had. He had known nothing else since the winter he was left on a temple step.
^You fought because you wanted to be the best.
^That was why you couldn't beat him.

hozoin_after_recognition: ^He was watching you. Still. The spear still raised but he was not attacking. He had felt something shift and he didn't know what to do with it.

c-0: ^He stared at you. No one had done this before. No one at this temple could beat him, and no one had ever simply stopped.
c-1: ^You raised your sword again — but something had changed in how you were holding it.
^You went three more exchanges. He beat you cleanly each time. On the third he swept your feet and you hit the ground.
^You looked up at him from the dirt.
^He was looking back at you with an expression you had not seen on him before.

hozoin_blind_charge: ^He saw it coming the way he saw everything coming — completely, instantly, without effort.
^You hit the ground. Hard. You didn't get up immediately.
^When you did the old man was there.
hozoin_surrender_outcome: ^Footsteps at the edge of the yard.
^The old man walked toward you slowly. He had been watching from somewhere you hadn't seen.
^"I was here the whole time," he said. He was not speaking to you. He was speaking to Inshun.
^Inshun said nothing. He was still watching you. Something was working behind his eyes.
hozoin_defeated_with_grace: ^The old man came from the edge of the yard. He said nothing for a moment. He looked at Inshun. Then at you.
^"Up," he said finally. He extended a hand.
hozoin_abbot_appears_late: ^The voice came from the edge of the yard. The old man walked toward you as if he had already been on his way before you fell.
^"I was nearby," he said — in the tone of a man accounting for a coincidence that wasn't one.
hozoin_abbot_speaks: ^He looked at Inshun for a long moment. Inshun looked back. There was something between them that had been accumulating for seventeen years.
hozoin_backstory_medium: ^"You saw something," the old man said. It was not a question.
^He glanced at Inshun, then back at you.
^"He was left here as an infant. In winter. This spear is the only family he has ever known."
^"What you felt when you looked at him — hold onto it. Come back in fourteen days with it intact and you might actually teach him something."
hozoin_backstory_minimal: ^"He was left on these steps as an infant," he said quietly. "This spear is the only family he has known."
hozoin_the_kill: ^The spear was in his hands and then it wasn't.
^After a long time the old man came from the edge of the yard. He walked slowly. He did not look at you.
^When he finally spoke it was very quiet.
^"I knew this was possible," he said. "I hoped otherwise."
^He was not angry. He was sad in a way that seemed to have no bottom to it.
^He stayed kneeling. He did not ask you to leave. He did not need to.
^The road continued. It always does. The temple bell did not sound as you went.
hozoin_death: ^Inshun stood over you. He was breathing normally. He lowered the spear.
^He had heard. He did not come.
^The temple continued. The boy grew into the man In'ei needed him to become — humbled eventually, by someone else, on some other day.
^The road you walked is still there.
hozoin_rematch_declared: ^Inshun had not moved. He was still watching you with that expression you couldn't read yet.

c-0: ^"Because it is all he has ever had."
^"He was left here as an infant. Every monk in this temple is a brother he has defeated. I am the only father he has known."
^"And I just wagered his life on a stranger from the road."
^"Fourteen days," he said again. And went back inside.

hozoin_the_woods: ^The forest was tall and the light came through it in long columns.
^Behind you the temple. Somewhere inside it a boy with a spear who was also thinking about fourteen days.
^You didn't know yet what you were training for.
^That was not quite true. You knew exactly what you were training for.
^You didn't know yet who you were trying to become.
hozoin_exit_no_rematch: ^The forest was there. You walked into it.
